title: ÖSD Zertifikat A1
description: Standard for entry and residence requirements for Austrian residency.

structure.name: Lesen (Reading)
structure.description: Read announcements, simple emails, and short information texts.

parts.title: Part 1: Emails/Messages
parts.description: Read 2 short emails or messages and decide if 5 statements are True or False.

parts.title: Part 2: Selective Reading
parts.description: Read 5 short descriptions of people/situations and match them to 5 corresponding locations/ads (Selective reading).

parts.title: Part 3: Signs/Advertisements
parts.description: Read notices, signs, or short advertisements and answer 5 multiple-choice questions (a, b, c).

parts.title: Part 1: Telephone Calls
parts.description: Listen to 5 short recordings (e.g., telephone calls) and answer 5 multiple-choice questions (visual/text). Heard twice.

parts.title: Part 2: Radio News
parts.description: Listen to a longer recording (e.g., radio news) and decide if 5 statements are True or False. Heard once.

parts.title: Part 3: Announcements
parts.description: Listen to 5 short announcements and match them to 5 pictures or categories. Heard once.

structure.name: Schreiben (Writing)
structure.description: Fill out personal information and write simple notes.

parts.title: Task 1: Form Filling
parts.description: Complete a form (e.g., registration) with 5 personal details based on a provided text.

parts.title: Task 2: Personal Note
parts.description: Write a short personal note/email (approx. 30 words) responding to 3 points in a prompt.

structure.name: Sprechen (Speaking)
structure.description: Talk about yourself and ask questions.

parts.title: Part 1: Introduction
parts.description: Introduction: Give relevant personal information based on keywords (Name, Age, Country, etc.).

parts.title: Part 2: Questions/Answers
parts.description: Ask and answer questions about personal life using visual prompt cards.

parts.title: Part 3: Requests/Suggestions
parts.description: Formula requests or make suggestions based on symbols/images; partner responds.

tips: Arrive at least 15 minutes before the exam starts.
Know the exam format perfectly (number of parts, timing).
Practice with official model tests to build confidence.
Master time management: never leave a question blank.
Reading/Listening: Read questions/options before text/audio starts.
Writing: Use standard connectors (weil, obwohl, deshalb) as shortcuts.
Speaking: Clear communication is better than 100% perfect grammar.
Speaking: Use safe phrases (Redemittel) to stay fluent.

resources.title: Information and Model Test
resources.description: Information and Model Test for ÖSD certificate A1 exam

title: ÖSD Zertifikat A2
description: Communicating on routine tasks and exchanges on familiar and common matters.

parts.title: Part 1: Global Understanding
parts.description: Read 5 short messages/emails and answer 5 multiple-choice questions (Global understanding).

parts.title: Part 2: Selective Reading
parts.description: Read newspaper ads or directory listings and match them to 5 specific requirements (Selective reading).

parts.title: Part 3: Detailed Understanding
parts.description: Read notices, signs, or short articles and identify 5 specific details (True/False or MCQ).

structure.name: Hören (Listening)
structure.description: Understand phone calls, radio, or direct spoken information.

parts.title: Part 1: Telephone Announcements
parts.description: Listen to 5 telephone announcements or messages and answer 5 multiple-choice questions (a, b, c). Heard once.

parts.title: Part 2: Interview/Dialogue
parts.description: Listen to a longer dialogue (e.g., interview) and decide if 5 statements are True or False. Heard once.

parts.title: Part 3: Radio Clips/News
parts.description: Listen to 5 radio announcements or bits of news and answer 5 multiple-choice questions (Selective listening). Heard twice.

structure.name: Schreiben (Writing)
structure.description: Write messages and fill out more complex forms.

parts.title: Task 1: Personal Email/Letter
parts.description: Write a short email/letter (approx. 40 words) for a given situation, covering 3 bullet points.

parts.title: Task 2: Form/Questionnaire
parts.description: Fill out a more complex form or questionnaire based on description (e.g., job application details).

structure.name: Sprechen (Speaking)
structure.description: Exchange information on personal and common topics.

parts.title: Part 1: Short Presentation
parts.description: Give a short presentation (approx 2 mins) about yourself and answer follow-up questions.

parts.title: Part 2: Dialogue
parts.description: Pick a prompt/topic and engage in a dialogue with your partner about your experiences.

parts.title: Part 3: Joint Planning
parts.description: Collaborate with your partner to plan something (e.g., a meeting or an outing) and reach a consensus.

tips: Arrive at least 15 minutes before the exam starts.
Know the exam format perfectly (number of parts, timing).
Practice with official model tests to build confidence.
Master time management: never leave a question blank.
Reading/Listening: Read questions/options before text/audio starts.
Writing: Use standard connectors (weil, obwohl, deshalb) as shortcuts.
Speaking: Clear communication is better than 100% perfect grammar.
Speaking: Use safe phrases (Redemittel) to stay fluent.

resources.title: Information and Model Test
resources.description: Information and Model Test for ÖSD Zertifikat A2 exam

title: ÖSD Zertifikat B1
description: Identical structure to Goethe-Zertifikat B1. Modular format.

parts.title: Part 1: Experience Reports
parts.description: Blog posts/reports - 6 True/False.

parts.title: Part 2: Newspaper Articles
parts.description: Newspaper reports - 6 MCQs (a, b, c).

parts.title: Part 3: Advertisements
parts.description: Match ads to situations (7 ads, 10 situations).

parts.title: Part 4: Forum Opinions
parts.description: Forum opinions - Identify authors' stance (Pro/Contra).

parts.title: Part 5: Instructions/Rules
parts.description: Rules/Instructions - 4 MCQs (a, b, c).

parts.title: Part 1: Short Announcements
parts.description: Short announcements - MCQ + True/False (5 items). Heard twice.

parts.title: Part 2: Presentation
parts.description: Lecture/Presentation - 5 MCQs. Heard once.

parts.title: Part 3: Conversation
parts.description: Dialogue between 2 people - 7 True/False. Heard once.

parts.title: Part 4: Radio Discussion
parts.description: Radio discussion - 8 speaker attributions. Heard twice.

parts.title: Task 1: Personal Email
parts.description: Personal email (80 words, 3 points).

parts.title: Task 2: Forum Post
parts.description: Forum post (80 words, expressing opinion).

parts.title: Task 3: Formal Email
parts.description: Formal email (40 words, e.g. apology).

parts.title: Part 1: Joint Planning
parts.description: Planning something together.

parts.title: Part 2: Oral Presentation
parts.description: Presentation on a topic (pros/cons).

parts.title: Part 3: Feedback/Questions
parts.description: Q&A session on the presentation.

tips: Arrive at least 15 minutes before the exam starts.
Know the exam format perfectly (number of parts, timing).
Practice with official model tests to build confidence.
Master time management: never leave a question blank.
Reading/Listening: Read questions/options before text/audio starts.
Writing: Use standard connectors (weil, obwohl, deshalb) as shortcuts.
Speaking: Clear communication is better than 100% perfect grammar.
Speaking: Use safe phrases (Redemittel) to stay fluent.

resources.title: Information and Model Test
resources.description: Information and Model Test for ÖSD Zertifikat B1 exam

title: ÖSD Zertifikat B2
description: General German for work or study. Advanced proficiency.

structure.name: Lesen (Reading)
structure.description: Understand complex texts and detailed information.

parts.title: Part 1: Global Understanding
parts.description: Read 5 short texts/articles and match to corresponding titles/headlines (Selective understanding).

parts.title: Part 2: Detailed Reading
parts.description: Read a longer magazine or newspaper article and answer 5 multiple-choice questions (Detailed reading).

parts.title: Part 3: Selective Reading
parts.description: Scan advertisements or specialized information and match correctly (Global reading).

parts.title: Part 4: Vocabulary/Grammar
parts.description: Read a text with gaps and fill them using correct prepositions or grammar (Advanced vocabulary/grammar).

structure.name: Hören (Listening)
structure.description: Understand radio shows, interviews, and public talk.

parts.title: Part 1: Radio Interview/Report
parts.description: Listen to a radio interview or report and answer 10 multiple-choice or short-answer questions. Heard twice.

parts.title: Part 2: Short Recordings
parts.description: Listen to 5 short recordings (news, weather, etc.) and answer 5 multiple-choice questions. Heard once.

parts.title: Part 3: Identifying Opinions
parts.description: Listen to a discussion and decide if 5 statements are True or False. Heard once.

structure.name: Schreiben (Writing)
structure.description: Express opinions on social or technical issues.

parts.title: Task 1: Personal Essay
parts.description: Write a detailed personal message or essay (approx. 200 words) on a provided topic.

parts.title: Task 2: Formal Message
parts.description: Respond to a professional or institutional email/request formally (approx. 120 words).

structure.name: Sprechen (Speaking)
structure.description: Advanced interaction and monologues. Typically 2 participants.

parts.title: Part 1: Detailed Presentation
parts.description: Give a detailed presentation (approx 4 mins) on a complex topic and answer questions.

parts.title: Part 2: Critical Discussion
parts.description: Engage in a critical discussion with your partner about a specific theme and exchange arguments.

parts.title: Part 3: Negotiating/Consensus
parts.description: Negotiate or solve a complex task together while defending your position.

tips: Arrive at least 15 minutes before the exam starts.
Know the exam format perfectly (number of parts, timing).
Practice with official model tests to build confidence.
Master time management: never leave a question blank.
Reading/Listening: Read questions/options before text/audio starts.
Writing: Use standard connectors (weil, obwohl, deshalb) as shortcuts.
Speaking: Clear communication is better than 100% perfect grammar.
Speaking: Use safe phrases (Redemittel) to stay fluent.

resources.title: Information and Model Test
resources.description: Information and Model Test for ÖSD Zertifikat B2 exam
